title: Project sharing, invites & cloning
description: Let an owner share a project with an existing user by email, or create a pending invite if the email is not yet registered. Support cloning a project ('send project to') so a recipient gets their own independent copy rather than co-editing the original. Real-time collaborative editing is explicitly out of scope.
notes: See docs/adr/0006-project-sharing-invites-and-cloning.md. Depends on EPIC-103 (ownership) and EPIC-102 (signup/login, for unregistered-email invite resolution). HARD BLOCKING DEPENDENCY: EPIC-100 (Catalogue globalization) must land first, per ADR-0001's revised sequencing — cloning should reference a shared global product, not deep-copy an item-scoped one; shipping this epic before EPIC-100 falls back to a deep-copy of item-scoped products, which ADR-0006/FEATURE-1052 both flag as a stopgap, not the intended end state. Clone copies items (and, only in the EPIC-100-not-yet-landed fallback, products) but never listings/matches/price history — the clone accumulates its own evidence. Deferred: real-time co-editing/multi-owner projects, granular per-item sharing, org/team sharing, pull-updates-from-original after clone.
